full_description:
I have thirty solid-modelling assignments, each made up of six individual questions. Every question ultimately needs its own fully‐defined 3D part or assembly created directly in CREO; no drawings or simulations are required—just native CREO files (.prt, .asm, etc.). All of the reference sketches, dimensions and brief problem statements are ready for you; they give clear geometry and tolerance information so you can concentrate on building clean, well-structured models.

Scope and workflow 
• Total models: 180 (30 assignments × 6 questions) 
• Software: CREO (current commercial release) 
• Timeline: 15 calendar days from project start 
• References: I’ll supply a zipped folder of PDFs and images for each assignment as soon as we agree on the contract.

Deliverables (per assignment) 
1. Six CREO native files, one for each question. 
2. A short text log summarising key features or assumptions, if any, made while modelling. 
3. All files named exactly as indicated in the reference sheet and packaged in a dedicated folder for that assignment.

Acceptance criteria 
• Files open error-free and regenerate without unresolved features. 
• Dimensions, fillets, drafts and other details follow the supplied references precisely. 
• Feature tree is logically organised and fully editable.

If you are confident working quickly and accurately in CREO and can commit to the two-week turnaround, let’s discuss the details so you can start right away.

full_description:
I need an experienced inspector to travel to the manufacturer’s facility and formally witness the complete test program on a three-phase induction motor. The scope is broad: mechanical inspection, electrical testing and a final performance evaluation must all be observed first-hand and documented.

On the mechanical side I want you to cover the basics—visual inspection and dimensional check—then go deeper with a full run test, document review and rotor-balancing verification. While the standard bearing and alignment checks may come up, the focus is on confirming that the vendor’s own procedures are followed and that every reading is captured accurately.

During electrical testing I expect you to monitor insulation resistance, winding resistance, high-potential and routine functional tests, noting any deviation from IEC 60034 or the client specification. Finally, the performance evaluation should confirm nameplate output against load, temperature rise and vibration figures.

Deliverables 
• Signed witness sheets from the manufacturer’s tests 
• Your independent inspection report, including photos, raw data and concise commentary on each section (mechanical, electrical, performance) 
• A summary of any non-conformities and recommended corrective actions 
• Digital copy delivered within 48 hours of test completion

The job is strictly on-site, so please state your location and availability. Familiarity with IEC/IEEE motor standards and clear technical reporting in English are essential.

Summary :
Inspection Scope for Three-Phase Induction Motor:

Document Review – MTCs, calibration certificates, approved drawings, procedures, and supplier certificates
Visual and Dimensional Inspection
Rotor Balancing Inspection
Routine Electrical Tests – winding resistance, no-load test, rotation direction, withstand voltage, and insulation resistance
Performance / Type Tests – impedance, breakdown torque, efficiency, power factor, and temperature-rise test
Polarization Index Test
Accessories and Instrumentation Check – PT-100, vibration sensors, and current transformers
Final Painting Inspection – paint thickness and adhesion

Country: Brazil
EQUIPMENT NAME/ 
TYPE OF MATERIAL:MOTOR MAI 710B 4.500kW 6.600V IC666 B3L 6P 50Hz
TYPE OF INSPECTION:
(BASED ON APPROVED ITP)

Av. Prefeito Waldemar Grubba, 3000 ‐ 89256‐900
•Jaraguá do Sul ‐ Santa Catarina ‐ Brazil

full_description:
I’m ready to hand over the entire content machine for my brand across Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok to a reliable team that can plan, produce, and publish professional-toned assets every week. The work spans short-form video (product demonstrations, customer testimonials, and behind-the-scenes snippets), eye-catching carousel graphics, and complementary single-image posts—all optimised for each platform’s specs and algorithms.

Here’s how I see the collaboration working. You’ll begin by mapping a monthly content calendar that aligns with my existing marketing goals. From there, you’ll script, shoot or source raw footage, edit it to a polished finish, add branded motion graphics, write concise captions with relevant hashtags, and schedule everything through a tool like Creator Studio, Buffer, or Hootsuite. Clear communication is key; I want status updates before each batch goes live and performance summaries so we can refine the strategy continuously.

Deliverables I expect each week 
• 3 short videos (15–60 sec) covering the three themes above 
• 2 multi-slide carousels (up to 10 panels) 
• 3 single-image posts with captions and hashtag sets 
• A scheduling sheet or confirmation links showing the content queued

All assets must remain on-brand, copyright-cleared, and formatted in 1080×1920 (Reels/TikTok) or 1080×1080 (feed) as appropriate. Source files (Premiere Pro/Final Cut projects, PSD/AI files, or Canva links) should be included so my team can archive and repurpose them later.

If your portfolio already showcases professional footage, slick editing, and consistent visual identity, let’s talk. I’m looking to start quickly and will prioritise teams that demonstrate an organised workflow and data-driven content decisions.

full_description:
We have a WordPress plugin that sells services on a CREDIT model. It is
already CODED and already TESTED on a local machine: 31 of 33 smoke tests
pass (the remaining 2 wait on OUR input data, not on defects) plus 6 of 6
payment-gate tests — including a real PayPal SANDBOX payment and refund.
We need an experienced developer to take it onto a real staging domain
and turn it into an accepted, live system:

 Stand up a fresh WordPress -> install & configure the plugin per the
 docs -> RE-RUN the provided acceptance suite on the real domain (30
 smoke tests in M1; the 3 dataset tests complete the 33 in M2; plus 6
 PayPal-gate tests) -> fix anything the real environment exposes -> go
 live on PayPal (one real transaction + refund) -> finish the operator
 docs -> hand over with a joint sign-off.

What the system does: a visitor clicks Buy now, creates an account at
checkout (if they don't have one — or signs in with Google), buys a
credit pack through PayPal; the plugin credits their wallet; they paste
a list and Submit; an external processing machine picks the job up over
a REST API and returns an Excel file; the buyer downloads it.

You do NOT build the plugin from scratch and you do NOT design anything —
the code exists with a complete English spec it was built to (DB schema,
API spec, UI contract, per-role flow maps). You configure, test, debug
and fix until it passes; the only thing you WRITE new is the operator
documentation at the final milestone. You also get a MOCK worker (Python)
that plays the processing machine, so you can run the whole buy ->
process -> download chain without our real system.

THE ACCEPTANCE BAR: the core measure is the test suite passing on
staging — plus the operator docs and the joint sign-off at the final
milestone. "Test-and-fix on the delivered code" is part of the contract:
a defect that makes a test fail is yours to fix whether it lives in the
plugin, the config, or the mock app; we don't argue about its origin. In
return, everything is specified and every test case is written for you —
you never guess a requirement.


SCOPE OF WORK

1. SITE SETUP on our staging domain from a clean WordPress: WooCommerce
 (guest checkout off, create-account-at-checkout on), PayPal Payments,
 an SMTP plugin (email must reach a real inbox), a self-hosted CRM
 plugin (opt-in checkbox), and a standard social-login plugin for
 Google sign-in (a NEW addition — you install from [login to view URL], pin
 its version, configure and smoke it). We supply every other plugin
 package at the tested versions.
2. PLUGIN INSTALL & CONFIG: activate (DB tables + seed + cron), create
 the fixed credit-pack products via the built-in product-editor tab,
 confirm the packages panel has no warnings, set the worker API key.
3. PAYMENT: on your own PayPal accounts until everything passes — see
 THE PAYPAL RULES below; it is the most unusual condition of this job,
 read it before you bid.
4. FULL ACCEPTANCE: re-run the smoke tests from the provided checklist
 (30 in M1; 3 dataset tests complete the 33 in M2) using the mock
 worker — from anonymous purchase to file download, admin/staff
 permissions, retention purge, email, CRM opt-in.
5. FIX UNTIL IT PASSES: any defect the tests expose is in scope; record
 each change in the deviation changelog. Fix rule: code that disagrees
 with the docs is a bug; a doc that looks wrong is a question for us. A
 failure caused by missing OWNER input (credentials, real seed, domain)
 is a blocker to log and raise — not counted against you.
 HOW CODE IS DELIVERED: every fix is COMMITTED to the private GitHub
 repository we provide, with clear messages — your commit history is
 how we review the work at each milestone. Code sent loose by zip or
 email is not accepted.
6. GO-LIVE + HANDOVER: PayPal Live (one real transaction + refund as
 evidence); WRITE the operator docs — 3 short guides (admin, staff,
 buyer) with real staging screenshots; the technical specs already
 exist, this final runbook is YOUR deliverable, no draft. Hand over
 per the joint checklist.

THE PAYPAL RULES (read before you bid)
 a) SANDBOX: your own sandbox account on the public staging domain
 (needed for webhooks); re-run the 6 payment-gate tests there. The
 full sandbox flow is already proven and a setup how-to is included.
 b) LIVE-PATH REHEARSAL: before we connect anything of ours, prove the
 LIVE path end-to-end on YOUR OWN live PayPal account using a
 temporary low-value test pack (e.g. $0.10–$1, staging only, deleted
 after): one real purchase -> correct credit -> one real refund.
 Evidence required (transaction IDs + matching DB rows + screen
 capture). Price these micro-transaction costs into your bid — they
 are cents, and they prove your work is done.
 c) OWNER SWAP AT GO-LIVE: only after the full suite passes and your
 live-path evidence is accepted does the owner connect the production
 PayPal account — the owner enters those credentials personally; you
 never see or handle them. Then the final go-live check runs on it.

NOT in scope: writing the plugin from scratch; designing UI/DB/API
(done); rewriting UI copy or brand touchpoints (a fixed contract);
coding the real data-processing worker (we run our own; a mock is
provided); sales/marketing pages, a custom payment gateway, a data
viewer, or analytics.


YOU RECEIVE (on signed contract)

- The full plugin source as a snapshot into a private GitHub repository
 we create and invite you into. No prior commit history — it starts
 with you (transparency, not a gap). You work IN this repo and deliver
 THROUGH it: the final repo state (fixed code + deviation log) is the
 code deliverable at each milestone. Plus the mock worker (Python,
 1-page guide).
- The complete English document pack the code was built to (product
 brief + 12-step journey; site/payment setup; DB/API architecture; UI
 spec; worker contract; acceptance checklist = 13-step deploy + 33
 smoke + 6 payment tests; per-role flow maps; joint sign-off checklist;
 mock-worker spec) + 3 browser-openable HTML contracts (2 UI screens
 with backend annotations + 1 design-token file) + 6 sample
 spreadsheets defining each service's exact output columns.
- Our local test evidence as a baseline (including the real PayPal
 sandbox run).
- A real staging domain + hosting access (you install the fresh
 WordPress; clean activation is a sign-off item) + all base plugin
 packages at tested versions (the Google social-login plugin is the one
 exception: new addition, you install and pin it). The package has NO
 credentials: you use your OWN PayPal accounts (see THE PAYPAL RULES)
 and your OWN Google OAuth test app for staging; the owner's PayPal and
 production Google OAuth enter only at go-live, connected by the owner.
- Note: the shipped service seed is a small placeholder on purpose; the
 real one is owner input, and its row count is NOT an acceptance
 condition.


MILESTONES & ACCEPTANCE

- M1 — ASSET LANE LIVE (30 of the 33 smoke tests): site on staging;
 sandbox purchase -> correct credit -> submit -> mock worker -> buyer
 downloads; 6/6 payment gates pass; every exposed defect fixed;
 live-path rehearsal on YOUR own PayPal account (with evidence); then
 go-live of the asset lane — one real transaction + refund on the
 owner's production account.
- M2 — DATASET/REVIEW LANE + docs: the 3 dataset tests pass (33/33);
 operator docs finished; joint sign-off. Code deliverable = the final
 repo state (fixed source + full deviation log), not a zip.
 In one line: M1 = the main lane earning for real; M2 = the review
 lane, the docs, and the signatures.
- MANDATORY EVIDENCE: every test group comes with a screencast or
 screenshot; money/credit/order tests also carry the transaction ID +
 matching DB row. No evidence = not accepted.


REQUIRED SKILLS

- MOST IMPORTANT: reading, debugging and fixing a WordPress plugin
 someone else wrote (hooks, custom $wpdb tables, capabilities, WP-Cron,
 REST API, admin-ajax). This is finish-existing-code, not greenfield.
- WooCommerce at the hook/lifecycle level (payment_complete, refund,
 product meta). No payment-gateway internals needed.
- Test discipline with evidence capture; atomic UPDATE, idempotency and
 race conditions understood so fixes land in the right place.
- WordPress security basics: nonces, current_user_can, prepared SQL,
 output escaping.
- Reads English technical docs and follows a contract exactly (ask when
 unsure — do not invent).
- Runs a simple Python app (the mock worker; guide provided).
- Git basics (clone, branch, commit, push) — all delivery is
 repository-based, never by zip.


TO APPLY, PLEASE SEND

1. One or two projects where you TOOK OVER existing WordPress/WooCommerce
 code to fix/finish (describe your role).
2. Concrete experience with custom DB tables + REST API in WordPress.
3. Your BID: a fixed price for M1, your timeline, and the warranty period
 you offer (e.g. 30 days) — after reading the above.
4. Confirm you can test with your OWN PayPal accounts as described in THE
 PAYPAL RULES (sandbox + a low-value live rehearsal). If you cannot,
 say so plainly and we will discuss before signing.
5. Start your reply with the word "CREDIT" so we know you read this fully.

Pricing: fixed price per milestone — YOU bid it (see #3 above); payment
is released per gate, never by the calendar. We work in English.

Contract terms, up front: code ownership (all work transfers on milestone
payment) and no-reuse of this project's source, documents and data — your
general skills and experience are of course not restricted. Signed before
source and docs are shared.

I’m looking for a polished mixed-media opening that instantly hooks viewers and moves them to explore my mobile app and its companion website. The piece should balance sleek motion graphics with brief live-action or stock clips so the result feels dynamic yet unmistakably professional.

Core goal 
Keep the audience engaged from the first second, guiding them toward installing the app or clicking through to the site. I’m not after a lengthy walkthrough—just an energetic “teaser” that sparks curiosity and establishes credibility.

What you’ll receive from me 
• Logo, colour palette, icon set and any screen recordings or hero images you might want to weave into the sequence. 
• A concise tagline and the key CTA we need to land.

• A 15-30 second intro delivered in 1080p (MP4 and MOV) plus the editable project files (After Effects, Premiere, or similar).

• A version sized for social posts (square or vertical) if the aspect ratio differs from the main cut.

Acceptance criteria 
1. Mixed-media approach is evident—clean motion graphics intercut with suitable live-action. 
2. The call-to-action and app name are clear and on-brand. 
3. Professional tone is maintained throughout—no cartoonish fonts or slapstick effects. 

If this sounds like your wheelhouse, let’s talk timing and any creative ideas you might have to elevate the engagement factor even further.

full_description:
I am looking for an experienced software developer to build a desktop application for my parcel business. This software is only for internal use by employees, not for customers.

The main goal is to simplify parcel booking, tracking, customer communication, and parcel delivery verification.

The software should be easy to use, fast, and require minimal training for staff.

1. Parcel Ticket Creation
Create a parcel booking within 30–60 seconds.
Auto-generate Ticket/Parcel Number.
Customer Name
Mobile Number
Receiver Mobile Number
Destination
Parcel Type
Weight
Charges
Payment Status (Paid/To Pay)

2. Parcel Status Management
Employees should be able to update parcel status with one click.

Parcel Dispatched
Parcel Arrived

Delivered

Daily
Weekly
Monthly
Date Range

Excel

You’ll start by getting familiar with my complete monthly budget—especially the day-to-day categories that matter most to me: groceries, restaurant spending, outings, rent and transportation. 

Once you understand those numbers, I need structured recommendations that do two things at once: reduce existing debt and free up cash for future investments. Short-term cost-cutting is less important than building a sustainable, growth-oriented rhythm for when bills are paid and new commitments are made. 

Key deliverables 
• A clear snapshot of current cash-flow timing (inflow vs. outflow). 
• A proposed calendar or schedule that shifts payables to maximize liquidity without disrupting essential recurring expenses. 
• Actionable, long-term strategies that help me chip away at debt while steadily carving out room for investment contributions. 

I’m open to any software you prefer, provided I can keep using it after hand-off. Accuracy, transparency and practical advice are paramount; fancy jargon isn’t. If you can turn numbers into a simple plan that fuels growth, let’s get started.

Our company’s HR mailbox—dedicated to incoming candidate applications—needs daily, hands-on care. I want every message quickly sorted, the genuine opportunities surfaced, and courteous replies sent without delay, so our hiring team can focus on interviews instead of inbox clutter.

Here’s the day-to-day flow I need you to own:

• Organize: Label and archive messages so only actionable items remain in the primary view. 
• Respond: Send friendly, on-brand acknowledgements, schedule-related follow-ups, and simple queries using templates I’ll provide (and any new ones you suggest).

Development of AI Agents for Automation of Reception and Receiving/Shipping Processes

We are seeking a qualified specialist or team to develop and implement Artificial Intelligence agents focused on process automation for a warehousing company. The main objective of this project is to optimize operations, reduce operational costs, and minimize human error through the application of advanced AI solutions.

The developed AI agents should be able to handle the ingestion and processing of various tasks, specifically the receipt and analysis of emails from clients and carriers. The core functionality will include transcribing and feeding data extracted from these emails directly into an API.

A crucial challenge is the variability in the format of received emails. The agents will need to be robust enough to extract information that may come from:

The email body (unstructured text).
Image attachments.
Excel spreadsheet attachments.
PDF document attachments (invoices, collection orders, etc.).
XML file attachments.

Development of AI Agents for Automation of Receiving/Shipping Processes. We are looking for a solution that offers high accuracy and adaptability to different customer communication patterns.

Detailed project documentation is available for interested parties.

full_description:
I want to recreate the pick-up-and-play magic of Soccer Superstar, but in the pool. The core loop is exactly the same: swipe to pass and shoot, clear short level scenarios, and advance through seasons. The difference is the sport—water polo instead of soccer—and the business model: no micro-transactions at all. Revenue and optional advantages come only from rewarded ads (for example, watching a spot grants an extra attempt at a tricky move or a retry on a multi staged level).

Game structure 
• Level-based progression: each cleared scenario advances the player through a club season, four seasons culminate in an Olympic tournament, then the cycle repeats with higher difficulty. With the difficulty reseting after 12 seasons and becoming a continuous loop
• Challenges inside a level mix classic score situations with two key objectives I need designed and balanced: beat the clock and pull off pre-defined special moves or tactics (e.g., no-look lob, quick inside pass). 
• Character customization is lightweight yet visible: players can pick tattoos plus change skin and hair color. No accessories for now, so UI art can stay minimal.

What I need from you 
1. A full mobile prototype (Unity or Unreal preferred, but I’m open as long as it runs smoothly on iOS and Android). 
2. Core swipe mechanics tuned for water resistance feel. 
3. Ad integration (rewarded + interstitial) wired to the extra-attempt flow. 
4. Season/level editor tools so I can add new scenarios without a full build. 
5. Simple, performant toon-style art for athletes, clubs, and Olympic venues. 
6. Basic soundscape: crowd, splash, whistle.

Acceptance criteria 
• APK/IPA builds reaching stable 60 FPS on mid-range devices. 
• First 40 levels fully playable, with correct challenge triggers and ad flow. 
• Customization menu functioning for tattoos and color swaps. 
• Clear documentation for adding clubs, seasons, Olympics cycle, and ads.

If you’ve shipped a swipe sports title or can show a fluid prototype of the core mechanic, let’s talk timelines and milestones.

full_description:
I have just launched a new e-commerce site and need a reliable partner to keep it running smoothly and growing for at least the next twelve months. 
Website performance 

Day to day, you will handle all bug fixes and feature tweaks as they arise while also running scheduled security audits and hardening the platform against threats. 
SEO 

On the growth side, I want a full-spectrum SEO program—on-page, off-page and technical—implemented and monitored continuously. That includes routine content optimisation, backlink outreach, site‐speed and crawlability improvements, plus clear monthly reporting on rankings, traffic and conversions.
Marketplaces

 My catalogue must stay perfectly synced with Amazon, Flipkart and Meesho, so regular product uploads, description updates and price checks across those marketplaces are part of the job. 
Promotion

Beyond organic work, I will lean on you for paid and organic promotion through Google and the major social media channels to keep the brand visible and sales moving. 
SMM/SMO

with an active social presence on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, WhatsApp and YouTube to turn that anonymity into awareness, traffic and, ultimately, sales. Here’s the core of the mission: • Optimise every key page and product for search so we rank quickly for our primary terms. • Develop and execute a steady stream of short, attention-grabbing videos—our audience engages best with moving content—to run across the five platforms above. • Grow follower counts and, more importantly, move a measurable share of them to the site each week. I’ll supply brand guidelines, past creative assets and access to the site’s CMS and analytics. You’ll bring the keyword research, video editing skills, social scheduling tools and the tracking know-how to prove what’s working.
Performance Matrix 

Each month I expect: • Fast turnaround on bugs or change requests • A documented security scan and any patches applied.

 • Updated or newly added SEO content & meta data 

• Fresh quality backlinks and disavow file maintenance

 • Technical fixes that improve Core Web Vitals 

• Marketplace listings fully up to date and error-free 

• A concise performance report with next-step recommendations If you are comfortable committing to a long-term arrangement and can demonstrate past success with similar e-commerce sites, I’m ready to start right away and set clear milestones together.

full_description:
Description:

I have a generic PySpark data-retention function (masking + deletion) running on Azure Databricks (Unity Catalog, Delta Lake) across ~14 business models / 80+ tables. I recently discovered that at least one table's config was built on an incorrect interpretation of the business requirement — the code was technically working correctly, but it was implementing the wrong rule, because whoever wrote the config confused two different columns as if they meant the same thing. This passed multiple rounds of testing because the tests were only checking "does the code do what the config says," not "does the config match the actual requirement."

I need someone to help me systematically catch and fix any other cases like this, and properly validate the function afterward.

What went wrong (real example, for context):

Business requirement: mask desk booking records based on the employee's HR status (Active/OnLeave → 5yr, all other statuses → 90 days), determined by joining the booking table to a separate HR/headcount table via email address.
What was actually implemented: masking based on the booking's own status field (confirmed/cancelled) — a completely different column, no join at all.
Both "worked" in the sense that the pipeline correctly executed whatever logic was configured — but the configured logic itself was wrong.

What I need:

Phase 1 — Requirement audit

For each table/model, compare the actual config (I'll provide the config file and the original written business requirement/spec docs) against what's really implemented in the code
Flag any mismatches between the documented rule and the actual logic (wrong column used, wrong join, wrong threshold, wrong condition, etc.)

Phase 2 — Fix the function/config

Correct the generic PySpark function and/or per-table config entries to match the real requirement
The generic function needs to support: date-threshold masking/deletion, optional joins to reference tables (including cases where the join columns have DIFFERENT names on each side, e.g. user_email joining to work_email_address), multi-condition splits (e.g. two different retention periods based on a status field), and both masking (replace with 'xxxxx'/NULL) and physical deletion

Phase 3 — Validate on multiple tables
Using this testing method (already agreed with my team lead):

Take a backup from the source/production table
Deliberately corrupt PII columns on rows that should qualify for masking/deletion (set to a dummy value)
Run the corrected function against this backup
Confirm the dummy values were correctly transformed, and confirm (via SQL EXCEPT comparison) that no other rows were affected
Repeat across 5-10 representative tables covering different logic patterns (simple date threshold, join-based, multi-condition splits, deletion-only)

Environment: Azure Databricks (Unity Catalog), Delta Lake, PySpark, Spark SQL. Tables range ~1M to 40M+ rows.

What I'll provide:

Current generic function code
Config file (JSON/Python dict) for all tables
Original written business requirement docs for each model
Access to the Databricks workspace (Prod Dev environment)

Deliverable:

A documented list of every mismatch found between requirement and implementation
Corrected function + config code
Completed validation results (with EXCEPT query proof) for at least 5-10 tables
A short written summary I can share with my lead explaining what was wrong and how it was fixed

Ideal skills: Databricks, Delta Lake, PySpark, Spark SQL, Unity Catalog, strong attention to detail for requirement-vs-implementation auditing, data QA/validation experience.

full_description:
Why Cybersecurity Is Important in 2026

In today's digital world, almost everything we do depends on the internet. We use online banking, social media, digital payments, cloud storage, and smart devices every day. While these technologies make life easier, they also create opportunities for cybercriminals to steal personal information and money. This is why cybersecurity has become one of the most important fields in the world.

Cybersecurity is the practice of protecting computers, networks, applications, and data from cyberattacks. These attacks can include hacking, phishing emails, ransomware, malware, and identity theft. A single successful attack can cause financial losses, damage a company's reputation, and expose sensitive customer information.

Businesses of all sizes are investing in cybersecurity because cyber threats are increasing every year. Banks, hospitals, schools, government organizations, and technology companies all require skilled cybersecurity professionals to protect their systems. As a result, cybersecurity has become one of the fastest-growing career fields, offering excellent salaries and long-term job security.

Individuals also have an important role in staying safe online. Using strong passwords, enabling two-factor authentication, updating software regularly, and avoiding suspicious links can significantly reduce the risk of cyberattacks. Basic digital awareness can prevent many common security problems.

Looking ahead, technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, cloud computing, and the Internet of Things (IoT) will continue to expand. At the same time, cyber threats will become more sophisticated. This means the demand for cybersecurity experts will continue to grow in the coming years.

In conclusion, cybersecurity is no longer optional—it is a necessity. Whether you are an individual protecting your personal data or a business securing valuable information, investing in cybersecurity is essential for a safer digital future.

full_description:
Freelance Graphic Designer for Website Banners and Social Media Materials

We are looking for a creative and reliable freelance graphic designer to support our ongoing marketing design needs.

Scope of Work

The selected freelancer will be responsible for creating various digital and print marketing materials, including but not limited to:

Website banners for desktop and mobile
Social media posts and advertisements
Promotional campaign visuals
Outlet posters and in-store promotional materials
Event or festive campaign designs
Flyers, brochures, and other marketing collateral
Resizing and adapting approved designs for different platforms and dimensions
Requirements
Strong graphic design skills with a good understanding of branding and visual hierarchy
Experience designing website banners, social media content, and print materials
Able to follow an existing brand identity and design guidelines
Able to propose creative concepts based on a campaign brief
Responsive, reliable, and able to meet agreed deadlines
Able to provide editable source files upon completion
Familiarity with Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Canva, or similar design software
Project Arrangement

This may begin as a project-based engagement, with the possibility of ongoing assignments for a suitable freelancer.

The number and type of design materials may vary depending on each campaign. Clear dimensions, content, references, and brand assets will be provided for individual assignments.

How to Apply

Please include the following in your proposal:

Your design portfolio, particularly website banners, social media designs, and promotional posters
Your estimated fee or pricing structure
Your typical turnaround time
The number of revisions included
The design software you use
Your availability for ongoing projects

Please only apply if you are comfortable handling both digital and print-related marketing materials and can maintain consistency across different campaign formats.

full_description:
I run an online store and need a simple, repeatable AI-driven workflow that lifts the product images and product descriptions already sitting on my site and publishes them to my Facebook page with minimal effort. Think “select item → press a button → ready-to-go Facebook post,” or, even better, a fully automated schedule that keeps my feed fresh without manual copy-and-paste.

Here’s what I expect from you: 
• A clear, step-by-step setup that shows me which AI or integration tools to use (e.g., Meta API, Zapier, Make, Python scripts, or any smarter alternative) and how to connect them to my existing videos, images, and text. 
• Live guidance—screen-share or short recorded tutorials—so I can repeat the process confidently on my own. 
• A lightweight script or automated flow that pulls the latest product images and descriptions, formats an engaging post, and pushes it straight to Facebook. 
• Proof that it works: at least one successful post created during our session, plus a checklist I can follow whenever I add new items.

If the same method can later expand to other content types, great—but my priority right now is getting product images and product descriptions online quickly and consistently. Let’s streamline this process together so I spend more time selling and less time posting.

full_description:
I need an experienced inspector to perform a technical inspection of a three-phase induction motor at WEG Equipamentos Elétricos S.A – Energy Division in Jaraguá do Sul, Santa Catarina, Brazil. The scope includes:

- Document Review (MTCs, calibration certificates, approved drawings, procedures, and supplier certificates)
- Visual and Dimensional Inspection
- Rotor Balancing Inspection
- Routine Electrical Tests (winding resistance, no-load test, rotation direction, withstand voltage, and insulation resistance)
- Performance/Type Tests (impedance, breakdown torque, efficiency, power factor, and temperature-rise test)
- Polarization Index Test
- Accessories and Instrumentation Check (PT-100, vibration sensors, and current transformers)
- Final Painting Inspection (paint thickness and adhesion)

The inspection will be based on the approved ITP. Deliverables include signed witness sheets from the manufacturer’s tests and an independent inspection report with photos and raw data. The requested inspection dates are from August 3 to August 7, 2026.

full_description: مركز لتدريس كورسات للغات...(الفرنسية والإنجليزية للمبتدئين)... مع ادخال مواد الكورسين ...اللغة الانجليزية وكورس اللغة الفرنسية مبدئيا ...و كورسات أخري مثل اماديوس... و جاليلو (كورسات الحجز والتزاكر)...تضاف لاحقا .... يمكن الدارس من ان يدفع الرسوم عبر التحويل البنكي... ويقوم مشرف الموقع من إرسال كلمة السر للدارس عبر الموبايل... ليدخل بها الكورس وتكون صالحة حتي ينتهي الدارس بطباعة شهادة النجاح او الاكمال من الموقع... يعمل على جميع الكمبيوترات وأجهزة الجوال.... ويمتاز بالسرعة... باللغتين العربي والانجليزي ...يحتوي على شريط اعلاني...و أيقونة دعائية يمكن تغيرها من وقت لآخر.مطلوب من المستقل تصميم الموقع بكل تفاصيله ..مع اللوقو ...والشهادة النهائية باللوقو ...تطبع اليا بعد الانتهاء بنجاح .
